mnemonics: YAML Ain't Markup Language (YAML) - Remember it's for data serialization, not document markup like HTML.
keyPoints: YAML is a human-readable data serialization standard
It is often used for configuration files and data exchange
Supports complex data structures like lists and maps
commonMistakes: Using tabs instead of spaces for indentation
Forgetting proper colon spacing in key-value pairs
Incorrect nesting of elements leading to parsing errors
